mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS momentum are not positive in greater than 50% of months, or if the mean return is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative 0.3 percentage point or larger increase in the unemployment rate over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the trigger fires fewer than 5 times in the test sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production and increases in the unemployment rate generate a joint recessionary signal that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone produces the same predictive power.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of months when both conditions are met, or if the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate regime, month-over-month increases in consumer prices signal successful reflation of nominal demand without immediate monetary tightening, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of low-rate months with rising CPIAUCSL, or if the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.2 during this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts indicate strengthening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns following positive HOUST momentum are not positive in greater than 50% of months, or if the mean return is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 1% signals an earnings recession as manufacturing utilization falls, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or if the signal accuracy is not significantly different from a coin flip (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive retail sales growth combined with a stable and accommodative federal funds rate below 0.5% generates strongly positive S&P 500 returns because demand expands without discount rate headwinds; this effect is absent when either condition holds alone.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of months when both conditions are met, or if the interaction term does not improve prediction R-squared by at least 0.02 over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP captures aggregate demand expansion that is not yet fully reflected in higher-frequency monthly data, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 surprises are not positive in greater than 50% of quarters, or if the mean return is negative over any 5-year rolling window.
